title: Data flow map
description: Trace personal data from collection through storage, processing, logs, sharing, export, deletion, and backups.
body: Use before modifying privacy-sensitive code or writing a review. Do not use to certify compliance; it is engineering evidence. Steps: list direct and indirect identifiers; identify collection points; enumerate stores, caches, queues, indexes, logs, and external destinations; identify retention and deletion triggers; then mark unknowns as blockers. Validation signals include file, schema, route, event, and log evidence. Risk boundary: unknown regulated data flow or external sharing requires human approval before implementation. Use when Privacy Risk Controls work touches personal-data classes, sinks, retention, consent, logs, prompts, and exports. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: follow the ordered review, edit, and validation sequence; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include data inventory, redaction tests, retention checks, export/delete tests, and approval evidence. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves PII disclosure, consent bypass, retention drift, and unapproved processor expansion, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
